message: I've been reading through some solid hurricane prep guidance, and honestly, it's way less overwhelming than I expected—mostly just common-sense stuff about knowing your area and having a plan. Turns out a lot of storm readiness overlaps with general emergency prep anyway, so if you've already got some basics down, you're further along than you think. What's one thing about storm season that always makes you go, 'Yeah, I should probably figure that out'?
